layman_headline: Crypto market is in deep fear territory — patience is the best trade right now.
layman_explanation: Bitcoin just fell below $60,000 and the entire crypto market is gripped by extreme fear (12 out of 100). Almost every coin is down significantly over the past week, and the charts are showing persistent selling pressure. In environments like this, catching a falling knife is dangerous — even coins that look cheap can get much cheaper. The best approach is to wait for signs of stabilization before committing capital.
layman_action: Do not buy anything right now. Keep your cash ready. Watch for Bitcoin to find a floor and hold it for 2-3 days before considering any new positions. Patience in extreme fear often leads to the best entries.
expert_reasoning: Every swing candidate fails the quality test right now. VELVET and GWEI have parabolic moves (+296% and +67% weekly) with overbought RSI — classic blow-off top risk. TRUMP and PEPE show RSI oversold but have deeply bearish chart structures (Three Black Crows, -1.4 bias scores) with no reversal confirmation. WLD dropped 27.5% with a Bearish Marubozu — pure sell conviction. SOL shows relative strength and a Doji candle hinting at potential indecision, but it's still below all moving averages with a -0.7 chart bias. With Fear & Greed at 12 and worsening, BTC dominance at 55.7% crushing alts, and major headwinds from Binance's EU exit and ETF sell walls, the risk-reward for any new position is poor. The correct play is to wait for a capitulation wick or confirmed reversal pattern.
risk_factors: BTC could break below $58K support triggering a cascade of liquidations and dragging all alts down another 15-25%
Binance regulatory crisis (EU exit, $400M outflows) could trigger broader exchange contagion fears
Fear & Greed at 12 and worsening — sentiment can stay irrational and push prices far below fair value
watch_list: BTC price action around $58,000-$60,000 — a daily close above $60K would be the first bullish signal
Fear & Greed index — any stabilization or uptick from 12 would suggest panic selling is exhausting
SOL's $66 support level — a bounce there with volume would create a proper swing entry setup
market_context: Bitcoin is below $60K heading for rare back-to-back quarterly losses, Fear & Greed is at 12 (extreme fear, worsening), and altcoins are being crushed with BTC dominance at 55.7%. This is a capital preservation environment, not a deployment environment.
context_banner: Extreme fear at 12/100 with Bitcoin below $60K and deteriorating sentiment — disciplined traders wait for confirmed reversal signals before deploying capital.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 12 (extreme fear) and worsening from 18 yesterday — panic selling is intensifying, not bottoming.
Bitcoin fell below $60K on track for rare back-to-back quarterly loss — a historically significant bearish milestone that signals deeper institutional caution.
Binance facing EU ban and $400M+ weekly outflows as MiCA deadline nears — major exchange risk adding selling pressure across the market.
Bitcoin's biggest ETF is becoming a sell wall — institutional flows turning negative removes a key demand pillar.
All swing candidates show either parabolic exhaustion (VELVET, GWEI) or bearish continuation patterns (TRUMP, PEPE, WLD) — no clean setups with favorable risk/reward above 2x.
